config: # IVP-T12 manual runbook execution history — 2026-04-21.
# Methodology: docs/plans/STAGE_T_IVP_T12_RUNBOOK.md
# Transcripts in logs/ack_stress_20260421_*.log
# Bug history (see commit 35f9591 and CHANGELOG 2026-04-21-003):
# Pre-fix
bw_khz: AO_Telemetry::interval_ms was hardcoded 200 ms in TelemAo_initial

config: # and only updated by the CLI 'r' key — SET_RADIO_CONFIG updated the config
# struct and the dashboard string but never touched the TX cadence gate.
# Every "10 Hz" config in the pre-fix rows was actually 5 Hz TX cadence.
# Post-fix findings (2026-04-21 evening re-run
# With actual TX cadence matching configured rate
# problem Batch B is designed to fix becomes clearly visible:
# Relative performance ranking (post-fix):
#   Best:  C2 (BW500/10Hz actual): 40% first-try
#   Worst: C1 (BW250/10Hz actual): 0% first-try
# C1's 0% is the architectural smoking gun: at BW250/10Hz the vehicle
# TX duty cycle is ~70% (70 ms airtime per 100 ms period). Any ACK the
# vehicle wants to send lands during its own nav TX and gets dropped
# per the C3-A2 TX-busy policy. Station's DISARM arriving during vehicle
# nav also drops. The path is nearly always blocked.
# C2 escapes this because BW500 airtime is ~15 ms — 15% duty cycle
# leaves 85 ms gaps per nav period for bidirectional commands.
# C0/C0P at BW125 are airtime-limited (~140 ms airtime) — the radio
# physically cannot sustain 10 Hz
bw_khz: so they run at ~5 Hz like pre-fix

config: # C0 but with the longer airtime-scaled retry timeout (910 ms vs old

bw_khz: which means fewer retry attempts per 10 s send interval.

config: # Pre-fix vs post-fix at C2: 83.3% -> 40% first-try. Not a regression —
# pre-fix C2 ran at 5 Hz (low duty)
# duty). Higher duty = more TX-busy collisions per ACK attempt.
# Conclusion consistent with pre-Batch-B expectations:
# The collision problem is real and severe at realistic flight-telemetry
# rates. T14 RxDone-anchored station TX + vehicle slot-aware ACK
# scheduling is the architectural fix. Neither driver hygiene (T11) nor
# airtime-scaled timeouts (this commit 35f9591) are sufficient on their
# own. They ARE prerequisites for T14 to function — you can't anchor
# windows correctly if your TX cadence doesn't match the config
# nor if TX-timeout is wrong for the current SF/BW.
# Future re-run: after AO_RfManager lands (Batch B) we should see:
#   - C2 first-try above 95% (Batch B gate) at actual 10 Hz
#   - C1 should also rise substantially (anchored TX fixes the starve
#     pattern even at higher duty cycle)
#   - C0/C0P will remain airtime-limited — the hardware constraint
#     isn't a scheduler problem. Users wanting 10 Hz at BW125 need
#     to reduce SF
# Static-bench caveat: all numbers here are room-temperature
# bench geometry. Real flight — thermal swing
# motion — will shift results. These measurements establish ordering and
# orders-of-magnitude
